content: When making infrastructure changes (ports, auth, networking) in Docker Compose, grep ALL compose file variants (data, services, modules, platform, infrastructure, production) — never assume one file covers it
When writing tests that use shared databases with cargo build slots, always scope operations by tenant_id or namespace — global queries cause flaky cross-test interference
Use entrypoint wrapper scripts to convert Docker secrets to environment variables at container startup — avoids changing application code while securing production credentials
When infrastructure auth changes break test suites, create a child bead for test fixes rather than expanding the parent bead's scope
Always reserve shared files via reserve-files.sh before editing when multiple agents may be active — prevents merge conflicts on compose files and shared configs
After rebasing or merging branches that touch migration files, verify _sqlx_migrations table consistency — removed or renamed migrations cause VersionMissing crash loops
When a prior bead misses file variants (legacy compose, production overlay), create a follow-up bead to sweep remaining files rather than reopening the closed bead
